description: The pluggable memory-engine protocol. agent-notes exposes a memory-provider surface (describe, ingest, operation_status, recall, forget) that the suite composes via subprocess — never by importing agent-notes internals. The suite's role is thin orchestration: configure the engine, check its health, and pin its version in SUITE.lock. Native is the default (no external dependency); Hindsight is a learned-context engine whose results are derived and untrusted.
repository_description: regista stores signed notes — the canonical, replay-verifiable record. Memory operations that touch durable storage write through regista.
engine_description: Derived and recallable context (embeddings, search indexes, synthesized summaries). Learned results are derived and untrusted; exact notes are canonical.
authority_semantics: exact notes are canonical; learned results are derived and untrusted
degradation_semantics: configured external outage is unhealthy; no silent fallback to native

state.description: Consistency state of the memory engine

indexing_backlog.description: Number of pending ingest operations not yet indexed

indexing_freshness.description: ISO timestamp of the last successful index operation

invariants: The suite composes the memory provider via subprocess (agent-notes memory-provider <verb> --json), never by importing agent-notes code
Native engine is the default and requires no external dependency; its health does not affect suite_ok
A configured Hindsight outage is unhealthy and DOES affect suite_ok — no silent fallback to native
Hindsight not configured (no HINDSIGHT_URL) is treated the same as native — no suite_ok impact
Learned results from the engine are derived and untrusted; exact notes in regista are canonical
The provider extension pin in SUITE.lock records the engine name, adapter version, protocol version, deployment mode, and support level
Provider drift (engine change, version change, mode change) is a named drift kind, not silence
